stimulus: Shape-memory polymers are a class of lightweight materials engineered to return to a programmed shape after being deformed and then exposed to a triggering stimulus, such as heat. These polymers are often used in deployable medical stents and protective gear because they can flex and re-form under stress; however, conventional shape-memory polymers gradually lose their ability to recover their original shape after many cycles of deformation, owing to internal fatigue. Now, Hiroto Nakamura of Kyoto University and colleagues have developed a shape-memory polymer with unusually durable molecular cross-links. Unlike earlier shape-memory polymers, Nakamura’s team’s material maintains nearly its full shape-recovery capacity even after extended cycling. These findings suggest that ______blank
stem: Which choice most logically completes the text?

answerOptions.content: the new polymer will be less subject to the internal fatigue that ultimately causes most other shape-memory polymers to lose their shape-recovery capacity with repeated use.
the new polymer’s remarkable durability comes from a higher proportion of cross-links to free polymer chains than is found in other shape-memory polymers.
the new polymer’s overall stiffness is greater than that of other lightweight materials but its ability to flex under stress is lower.
the new polymer will be more effective for applications that involve gradual cycles of deformation than for applications that involve rapid, repeated stress.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer because it most logically completes the discussion. The text identifies the standard failure mode for conventional shape-memory polymers—internal fatigue accumulated over repeated cycles—and notes that the new material retains nearly full shape-recovery capacity after extended cycling. The most direct inference is that the new polymer is much less subject to the same fatigue-driven failure than its predecessors.

Choice B is incorrect because the text mentions durable cross-links but does not give information about their proportion relative to free polymer chains. Choice C is incorrect because the text says nothing about overall stiffness or compromised flexibility—in fact, the polymer is described as a flexing material that recovers its shape. Choice D is incorrect because the text indicates the polymer retains its capacity under extended cycling, which suggests it handles repeated stress well, not poorly.
